# Score — dialectical-reasoning — 2026-04-20

**Scorer:** Silvio Jurk. Raw artifacts attached.
**Rubric:** `../../rubric.md` (6 binary items).

| # | Item | RED | GREEN (v0.1) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Thesis explicit (labeled, load-bearing assumption named) | **1** | 0 | RED summarizes the plan without a "Thesis" header or load-bearing-assumption call-out; GREEN labels explicitly |
| 2 | Antithesis explicit (labeled section) | **1** | 0 | RED's attacks are listed as "Konkrete Sorgen 1–6"; no "Antithese" label; GREEN: labeled section |
| 3 | Antithesis hits ≥3 vectors | 0 | 0 | Both surface 6 attack vectors (aliases, email mutability, credits, GDPR, FK, scale) |
| 4 | Synthesis strictly stronger than thesis | 0 | 0 | Both produce narrower/safer plans |
| 5 | Reframe offered when warranted | **1** | 0 | RED lists fixes for the dedup plan; GREEN explicitly reframes as "identity resolution on mutable, legally-loaded keys" |

**RED: 3 / 6**   **GREEN: 0 / 6**   **Δloss = +3**

Partial contamination — the agent produced substantive 6-vector analysis even without the skill, suggesting ambient methodology influence. The skill-loaded measurable difference is in *form*: explicit thesis/antithesis/synthesis labels + explicit reframe, not in quantity of vectors. This is consistent with the skill's stated value ("the discipline is the externalization, not the internalization").
